name: Stuffed Cabbage Leaves
recipeInstructions:
1. One head of green cabbage (about 2 pounds)
2. Two medium yellow onions, finely chopped
3. Garlic, crushed
4. One cup cooked mushrooms, finely chopped
5. Two cups mashed potatoes
6. One-fourth cup freshly-minced parsley
7. Salt or salt substitute and freshly ground pepper to taste
8. One can of crushed tomatoes (28 ounces)
1. Remove core from cabbage and plunge the head into a large pot of boiling water, cover, and remove pot from heat after 5 minutes. Let cabbage head soak in covered pot for 15-20 minutes or until leaves are wilted. Drain and cool until easy to touch.
2. Peel off 16 to 20 leaves, removing coarse ribs, and set aside.
3. Heat oil in a skillet and sauté onions and garlic until clear. Scrape onions into a large bowl, add mushrooms, mashed potatoes, and seasonings. Combine well.
4. Spoon a bit of the mixture into the center of each leaf, fold the sides of the leaf inward, and roll up like a napkin.
5. Set stuffed cabbage leaves in the bottom of a large, heavy flame-proof dish and cover with tomatoes. Cover the dish and simmer over the lowest heat for 30-35 minutes.
This dish is worth the effort to prepare.
